rationale: The page provides specific historical and cultural details about the Natyashastra, including its authorship, time period, alternative names, and its significance as a foundational text for Indian drama and music classification.
cleanedText: About Natyashastra (Science of Dramaturgy) - Also referred to as 'Fifth Veda', it was written by Bharatamuni in Sanskrit and was codified around the 2nd century BCE. - It comprises comprehensive set of rules that define nāṭya (drama), abhinaya (performance), rasa (aesthetic experience), bhāva (emotion), saṅgīta (music), etc. to create Rasa. - It is an epitome of Nāṭyaveda (also known as the Gāndharvaveda). - Natyotpatti, meaning "birth of performing art," refers to the origin story of the Natyashastra. - It is the first text available on the classification of India musical instruments.
